verses.1: Dead flies turn a perfumer’s oil rancid, so a little foolishness is more potent than wisdom and honor.
verses.2: A wise person’s heart directs him toward the right, but the foolish person’s heart directs him toward the left.
verses.3: Even when the fool walks along the road, his sense is lacking, and he demonstrates to everyone that he is a fool.
verses.4: If the ruler’s temper rises against you, do not abandon your place, because composure puts great offenses to rest.
verses.5: There is an evil I have seen under the sun, like a mistake that proceeds from the ruler:
verses.6: foolishness is set in many exalted places while the rich sit in humble places.
verses.7: I have seen slaves riding on horses and princes walking like slaves on the land.
verses.8: One who digs a pit may fall into it, and a serpent may bite one who breaks through a wall.
verses.9: One who quarries stones may be hurt by them, and one who splits logs may be endangered by them.
verses.10: If the axe is dull and he does not sharpen its edge, then he must exert more strength. Wisdom has the advantage of bringing success.
verses.11: If the serpent bites before being charmed, there is no benefit for the charmer.
verses.12: Words from the mouth of a wise person are gracious, while the lips of a fool consume him;
verses.13: the beginning of his talking is foolishness, and the end of it is evil insanity.
verses.14: Yet the fool multiplies words. No person knows what will happen, and who can tell him what will come after him?
verses.15: The labor of a fool makes him so weary that he does not even know how to go to a city.
verses.16: Woe to you, land whose king is a boy, and whose princes feast in the morning.
verses.17: Blessed are you, land whose king is of nobility, and whose princes eat at the appropriate time—for strength and not for drunkenness.
verses.18: Through extreme laziness the rafters sag, and through idleness the house leaks.
verses.19: People prepare a meal for enjoyment, wine makes life joyful, and money is the answer to everything.
verses.20: Furthermore, in your bedroom do not curse a king, and in your sleeping rooms do not curse a rich person; for a bird of the sky will bring the sound, and the winged one will make your word known.
